title: partial copy
text: partial copy Partial copy is the mechanism for transferring XML fragments between components and XML streams, without markup characters such as angle brackets (< and >) and ampersand (&) being converted into XML entities (such as < and & respectively). This mechanism is known as partial copy because Uniface copies parts of an XML stream directly to (or from) a component field. Related Links XML entity Named replacement texts defined in the XML recommendation, DTD, or XML document. Entities start with an ampersand (&), followed by the entity name, and end with a semicolon (;). For example, the entity < is defined in the XML Recommendation www.w3.org/XML and is replaced with < when an XML document is parsed. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
